title: angelica cryptotaeniifolia var. cryptotaeniifolia
intro: Angelica cryptotaeniifolia var. cryptotaeniifolia, also known as Korean angelica or dong quai's relative, is a species of plant in the family Apiaceae. It is a medicinal plant traditionally used in East Asian countries to treat various health conditions, including menstrual disorders, digestive issues, and fever. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its essential oils, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. Korean angelica is used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also consumed as a raw or dried vegetable in traditional Korean cuisine.
